mission: Render 100k+ asteroids client-side, frustum + distance culled, at a smooth frame rate, with zero per-asteroid server work.

target.clientBenchmark: bun scripts/bench/client-render.ts (boots server+vite+chromium, reads window.__HAYSTACK_RENDER_STATS__ + renderer.info)
target.serverBenchmark: bun scripts/bench/world-stream.ts (must stay flat across renderLimit)
target.parityBenchmark: bun scripts/bench/parity-check.ts (client/server field generators must agree)
target.notes: Primary, robust pass signals are the deterministic app-exposed counts (submitted instances after culling, draw calls, rendered triangles) — NOT noisy headless-swiftshader FPS. Frame-time thresholds are knobs and generous. All thresholds are knobs; do not weaken a condition to turn an item green.

baseline.realHardware: On a 2025 MacBook Pro, rendering 100k asteroids ran at < 1 FPS — the render path is the bottleneck, not the server.
baseline.culling: Single InstancedMesh with one bounding sphere => Three.js culls all-or-nothing; effectively NO per-instance frustum culling.
baseline.serverIndependence: world-stream bench measured publishAll p50=0.14ms p95=0.18ms, ~1.5KB/tick, IDENTICAL at renderLimit 2000 vs 100000 (field is client-derived). This is already correct and must be preserved.

items.name: Client-render benchmark harness
items.spec: Build scripts/bench/client-render.ts: a headless Playwright harness (reuse tests/e2e patterns) that boots `bun src/server/main.ts` at a chosen HAYSTACK_RENDERED_LIMIT, boots vite, loads the client in chromium, flies/positions the ship into the field, and prints JSON metrics per renderLimit. Metrics MUST include: derivedAsteroidCount, submittedInstanceCount (instances actually drawn after culling), drawCalls (renderer.info.render.calls), renderedTriangles, fieldDeriveMs, worstCellCrossFrameMs, medianFrameMs, and a 'cameraFacingEmpty' submittedInstanceCount. Expose the per-frame stats from the app via window.__HAYSTACK_RENDER_STATS__ (set from the render loop). Also include a fast in-process micro-bench of deriveVirtualField at 100k (no browser). PASS: the harness runs green and prints the metrics for renderLimit in {2000, 100000}; baseline numbers recorded in progress-client.txt.

items.name: Baseline + server-independence confirmation
items.spec: Using C0's harness, capture the current-HEAD client baseline at renderLimit 100000 (expected: catastrophic — ~100k instances submitted, unbounded triangles, multi-hundred-ms frames / sub-1-FPS as seen on real hardware) and at 2000. Re-run scripts/bench/world-stream.ts at limit 2000 vs 100000 to confirm server publishAll p95 and bytes/sec/peer are within ~15%. PASS: a baseline table (client @2k, client @100k, server @2k, server @100k) is recorded in progress-client.txt with real numbers, and server independence is confirmed numerically.

items.name: Remove the 2000 client render cap
items.spec: Lift the hard ASTEROID_CAPACITY=2000 ceiling so the client can hold/render the full derived set up to 100k+ (dynamic capacity sized to the derived count, or chunked meshes). The default HAYSTACK_RENDERED_LIMIT may stay low for normal play, but with it set to 100000 the scene must contain all derived rocks with NO 2000 truncation. PASS: client-render benchmark at renderLimit=100000 reports derivedAsteroidCount >= 100000 AND the instanced rendering holds the full set (no Math.min(_, 2000) truncation); existing tests still green.

items.name: Real per-instance frustum culling
items.spec: Only asteroids inside the camera frustum are submitted to the GPU each frame — implemented via per-instance frustum testing, spatial chunking into individually-cullable sub-meshes (e.g. per-cell groups), or a GPU/compute cull — NOT the current single-bounding-sphere all-or-nothing. PASS: at renderLimit=100000 with the camera facing the field, submittedInstanceCount is far less than derivedAsteroidCount (only what is actually in view, e.g. < ~25% and bounded by FOV/draw-distance), AND with the camera facing empty space / rotated away, submittedInstanceCount is approximately 0; drawCalls stays a small bounded constant in both cases. A selected/known/seeded rock that is in view is never wrongly culled.

items.name: Amortized instance build — no cell-cross hitch
items.spec: Building and uploading instance matrices/attributes for up to 100k rocks must not block a frame on cell crossings (a 5 km/s player re-pages cells constantly). Amortize across frames, build only newly-entered chunks, reuse buffers, or build off the hot path. PASS: at renderLimit=100000, worstCellCrossFrameMs in the benchmark is under the budget knob (default 50 ms) and steady-state medianFrameMs is well under it; no per-frame full 100k rebuild.

items.name: Bounded triangles via distance culling / LOD
items.spec: Rendered triangle count must be bounded regardless of derived count: cull beyond a max draw distance and/or drop far rocks to cheaper geometry/impostors, while preserving near-field visual fidelity and not popping in the player's immediate surroundings. PASS: at renderLimit=100000, renderedTriangles is bounded (does not scale ~linearly to 100k * full-geometry-tris; stays under a knob, e.g. comparable to rendering a few thousand near rocks at full detail) AND a near-field screenshot is visually intact (e2e screenshot still passes).

items.name: No functional/quality/parity/server regression
items.spec: PASS: `bun run verify` green (minus documented pre-existing fails); e2e screenshot/multiplayer/ui green; `bun scripts/bench/parity-check.ts` green (client/server field generators still agree exactly); a checked validation at renderLimit=100000 confirms mining, a surface/pocket scan, asteroid/deposit discovery, selection + overview + show-info on a rock, and multiplayer remote-ship sync still work; AND world-stream bench confirms server cost is STILL independent of renderLimit (within ~15%, 2000 vs 100000).

items.name: Before/after summary
items.spec: PASS: progress-client.txt contains a before/after table: client render cap (2000 -> dynamic/100k+), culling (none/all-or-nothing -> frustum + distance/LOD), submittedInstanceCount @100k (before ~100k -> after the small in-view count), drawCalls, renderedTriangles, worstCellCrossFrameMs, medianFrameMs (before sub-1-FPS -> after), and server publishAll p95 (unchanged). State the achieved improvement honestly; if a target wasn't fully met, give the measured number and the binding limit.
